news.title: Wallabies vs Ireland live: Captain’s try scrubbed out in contentious obstruction call

sections.heading: Murrayfield hosts Fijian 'home' fixture
sections.body: Fiji prepares for a home game in Edinburgh as the squad continues its northern hemisphere residency. With Suva's HFC Bank Stadium under the tournament's 25,000-capacity minimum, the Flying Fijians are using Scottish Gas Murrayfield to maximize financial returns. Chairman John Sanday noted the tournament is a "transformational opportunity for us" to generate revenue for domestic high-performance investment while showcasing culture through a fan zone.

sections.heading: Squad focuses on tactical recovery
sections.body: Squad conditioning and set-piece stability have become the primary focus during training sessions in the United Kingdom. Following the physical toll of consecutive fixtures against Wales and England, the coaching staff is prioritizing recovery protocols to ensure the starting XV maintains match fitness. There have been no reported injuries in the local press since the round two defeat at Everton's Hill Dickinson Stadium.

sections.heading: Townsend manages triple injury blow
sections.body: Scotland must navigate a triple injury blow after losing three Glasgow Warriors following their loss to South Africa. Gregor Townsend will be without Kyle Steyn, Alex Samuel, and vice-captain Rory Darge, who is sidelined with a concussion. Despite the absences, the Scottish camp remains confident, with Matt Fagerson insisting the group is building something special as they look to bounce back from their defeat.

sections.heading: Fiji seeks first championship points
sections.body: Fiji goes into the weekend bottom of the log after two consecutive losses left them with a -80 points difference. A positive result at Murrayfield is essential to avoid being adrift at the foot of the table heading into the latter stages of the championship. With zero points from their opening fixtures, the side needs a significant shift in discipline to secure a result.

sections.heading: Quirke drafted into squad as Borthwick weighs changes
sections.body: Scrum-half Raffi Quirke has been called into the England squad to replace Alex Mitchell. With pressure mounting on Steve Borthwick to reward recent form, debate continues over whether hat-trick hero Henry Pollock will earn his first start. Coach Borthwick has yet to confirm his selection, though competition for positions at scrum-half and in the back row remains intense following the lopsided victory over Fiji.

sections.heading: Mitchell ruled out following Fiji win
sections.body: Alex Mitchell is confirmed to be out of contention for Saturday’s Test after suffering a hamstring injury during the win against Fiji. There is no further injury news in today’s coverage regarding the remainder of the squad. The team is continuing its preparations in anticipation of the upcoming fixture at Estadio Único Madre de Ciudades.

sections.heading: Hostile atmosphere expected in Santiago del Estero
sections.body: England are bracing for a feisty atmosphere in Argentina. Captain Jamie George described the country as a "challenging place to go" and noted that the environment is both hostile and different. Bad blood remains from last year’s encounter, where Argentina coach Felipe Contepomi accused England of aggressive tactics. The squad is also mindful of the fervent local interest surrounding concurrent international sporting events.

sections.heading: England look to maintain momentum in Round 3
sections.body: England enters the third round sitting 7 of 12 on the log. Following a win and a loss in their opening two matches, Saturday’s result against Argentina is critical for climbing the table. Improving on their current points difference of +41 will be a primary objective as the team seeks to build upon the momentum generated by their dominant 73-8 performance last weekend.

sections.heading: Selection focus shifts to Japan challenge
sections.body: Following their impressive 42-26 victory over Australia last round, France are back on the training pitch to prepare for their third Nations Championship fixture. With the squad showing attacking rhythm in Brisbane, staff are evaluating rotations to maintain intensity. The coaching group is expected to finalize their match-day squad later this week as they look to build upon their current momentum in the competition.

sections.heading: Penaud remains sidelined for campaign
sections.body: Damian Penaud is confirmed to miss the remainder of the Nations Championship. The winger sustained a calf injury during the tournament-opening loss and is unavailable for the clash with Japan. There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage regarding other members of the squad, as the team focuses on rehabilitation and conditioning sessions ahead of the upcoming Test match.

sections.heading: Japan prepare for third round test
sections.body: Japan enter this fixture looking to test themselves against the French side after a challenging opening period in the inaugural tournament. While recent historic meetings between the two nations saw France secure emphatic victories, Japan continue to position themselves as an outfit that plays without fear. Their ability to surprise established nations remains a key consideration for the French defensive coaches this week.

sections.heading: Maintaining momentum in the standings
sections.body: France currently sit 4th on the log with one win and one loss from their opening two fixtures, accumulating 74 points for and 60 against for a plus-14 difference. Securing a victory against Japan is critical to ensure they remain in contention for the finals weekend in November, as the competition for top spots in the northern hemisphere table continues to intensify.

sections.heading: Selection options expand as squad hits full fitness
sections.body: Ireland’s coaching staff faces a welcome selection dilemma for the final Test. All 36 squad members participated fully in Tuesday’s training session at King’s College. Winger Rob Baloucoune has returned to training following a hamstring issue, joining the group alongside other returning players. Assistant coach Andrew Goodman noted that recent performances from players like Jimmy O’Brien have created healthy internal competition for the match-day squad.

sections.heading: Clean bill of health reported for Auckland
sections.body: The camp is reporting a clean bill of health following the conclusion of the second round. Rob Baloucoune is fit and in contention, having recovered from the hamstring injury that sidelined him for the opening two fixtures. Jeremy Loughman has also returned to full training after missing the Japan Test due to a head injury. No new injury concerns have been reported in the latest squad updates.

sections.heading: All Blacks defend historic Eden Park fortress
sections.body: New Zealand enters this fixture with a formidable record at Eden Park, having remained unbeaten at the venue for 52 consecutive matches dating back to 1994. Head coach Dave Rennie has stated that his side is well aware of Ireland’s ability. The All Blacks have maintained their momentum in the championship by overcoming France and Italy in their previous outings during this summer series.

sections.heading: Final push for the inaugural tournament title
sections.body: Ireland heads into this final round with two wins from two matches, placing them firmly in the mix at the top of the log. Facing the All Blacks represents the ultimate test of the campaign. Having already secured victories over Australia and Japan, the squad aims to finish the season with what coach Andy Farrell describes as a performance that they can be really proud of.

sections.heading: Cannone ruled out for four matches
sections.body: Italy will be forced to reshape their forward pack for the upcoming fixture after second row Niccolò Cannone received a four-match suspension. The ban follows his red card during the Round 2 defeat to the All Blacks in Wellington. With the lock now unavailable for selection, the coaching staff faces the immediate challenge of rebalancing the tight five for this weekend’s crucial encounter.

sections.heading: Squad focus shifts to recovery
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today’s coverage following the conclusion of the second round. The squad is currently focusing on their standard recovery protocols and physical conditioning as they transition into preparations for the trip to Perth. Management is expected to monitor the group’s overall fatigue levels closely as they attempt to integrate new personnel into the starting pack.

sections.heading: Wallabies under pressure in Perth
sections.body: Joe Schmidt remains under significant scrutiny as Australia attempts to arrest a slump that has seen them lose 9 of their last 10 Test matches. Reports indicate the Wallabies are potentially dealing with fitness concerns, with at least one key playmaker currently in a race to be declared available for Saturday. The hosts are desperate to regain momentum against an Italian side with recent pedigree.

sections.heading: Searching for a first tournament win
sections.body: Italy sits 11th on the log with zero points after two rounds and a points difference of -47. Following losses to both Japan and New Zealand, the Azzurri are targeting a turnaround to validate their development. A victory in Perth would represent a significant milestone in this campaign, as Italy seeks to maintain the competitive form shown in flashes during their last outing.

sections.heading: Squad depth tested as focus shifts to clinical edge
sections.body: Forwards coach John Dalziel has confirmed that Scotland’s internal focus is now firmly on finishing opportunities inside the red zone. Despite a high-scoring defeat in Pretoria, the coaching staff remains positive about the team’s trajectory. Dalziel noted that the side is no longer satisfied with praise alone, stating, "We’re very clear on who we are and what we are. We just need to be more clinical."

sections.heading: Triple blow ruled out for weekend fixture
sections.body: Scotland have confirmed that Kyle Steyn, Rory Darge, and Alex Samuel are unavailable for this weekend. Darge is sidelined with a concussion sustained against South Africa, while Steyn is managing a foot injury. Samuel, who withdrew from the previous matchday squad, is set for knee surgery and faces a multi-week recovery. No further squad additions have been made following these confirmed absences.

sections.heading: Fiji expected to bring intensity to Murrayfield
sections.body: Fiji are currently fulfilling a schedule of 'home' matches in the UK, playing out of facilities that meet tournament capacity requirements. Coaching staff have braced for a significant reaction from the visitors, who are looking to bounce back following their recent outing against England. Fiji’s campaign is part of a wider effort to drive financial growth for their home union through these international fixtures.

sections.heading: Building momentum in the Nations Championship
sections.body: Scotland sits 6 of 12 on the tournament log with 1 win from 2 matches and a point differential of -5. With only one round remaining in this block, a victory against Fiji is vital to climbing the table. The team is keen to convert their recent expansive performances into a result, aiming to move up from their current position before the conclusion of the weekend's action.

sections.heading: Squad depth tested as captaincy doubt lingers
sections.body: Wales have moved to bolster their ranks ahead of the encounter with the world champions, calling up hooker Elliot Dee and versatile forward Freddie Thomas. The squad remains in a period of uncertainty regarding skipper Dewi Lake, who is set to miss the upcoming Test. Forwards coach Danny Wilson maintains the group is relishing the challenge, noting that the team will be “bouncing for opportunity” in Durban.

sections.heading: Lake facing time on the sidelines
sections.body: Dewi Lake is the primary concern for the coaching staff, as he is set to miss his first game under Steve Tandy. There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage regarding other members of the squad. The team continues to prepare for the physical requirements of facing the Springboks following their recent fixture in Argentina.

sections.heading: Springboks hand debuts to quartet
sections.body: South Africa enters the contest with momentum, sitting among the three teams maintaining a perfect Nations Championship record. Head coach Rassie Erasmus has named four debutants in his side, including Sharks fly-half Vusi Moyo. Pieter-Steph du Toit will again lead the world champions as they look to extend their unbeaten run against a Wales side currently seeking to regain their footing.

sections.heading: Targeting a climb up the log
sections.body: Wales sit 8th in the 12-team log after their opening two rounds, holding a narrow positive points difference of +1 following a victory and a loss. With only one win recorded so far, the fixture in Durban represents a significant test against the tournament leaders. Securing points on the road is essential for Wales to improve their standing as the competition approaches the midway point.

sections.heading: Preparations intensify as atmosphere builds
sections.body: The squad is focusing on their final preparations for Saturday’s visit from England. With the international press highlighting the intense rivalry surrounding these fixtures, the Pumas are working behind closed doors at their training base. There has been no formal announcement of the starting lineup or match-day squad from head coach Felipe Contepomi at this stage of the week.

sections.heading: Conditioning focus in camp
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today’s coverage regarding the Argentina camp. The team has been prioritizing recovery sessions and structured conditioning drills following their victory over Wales. The coaching staff is using this window to manage player workloads and ensure the squad remains in peak physical condition for the high-intensity challenge expected when England arrives in Santiago del Estero.

sections.heading: England bolster ranks ahead of arrival
sections.body: England have called up scrum-half Raffi Quirke to replace the injured Alex Mitchell, who sustained a hamstring issue during their win over Fiji. Captain Jamie George has described the atmosphere in Argentina as “hostile” and “different,” while coach Steve Borthwick continues to face external pressure to promote hat-trick hero Henry Pollock to the starting lineup for this crucial Nations Championship fixture.

sections.heading: Building momentum on the table
sections.body: Argentina currently sit 5th on the log with one win and one loss from their opening two matches, holding a points difference of +5. With 73 points scored and 68 conceded, a victory this weekend would provide a significant boost in the standings. Consistency is the primary goal for the Pumas as they look to capitalize on their recent result against Wales.

match.venue: Estadio Único Madre de Ciudades, Santiago del Estero

sections.heading: Schmidt looks to youth for Italy
sections.body: Wallabies coach Joe Schmidt has resisted calls to bring in veteran playmakers for this week’s match in Perth. Despite recent struggles with inexperienced halves in the loss to France, Schmidt has opted to stick with his current squad rather than issuing an SOS to seasoned options. The team is currently assessing the fitness of a key playmaker who remains in the race to return.

sections.heading: Key playmaker nearing potential return
sections.body: The Wallabies are hopeful of a boost this weekend as one of their primary playmakers pushes to be available for the Italy clash. While the team was forced to navigate the previous two matches without this player, the medical staff is continuing to monitor his recovery. There is no other fresh injury news reported for the wider squad following the round two defeat.

sections.heading: Cannone ruled out for Azzurri
sections.body: Italy will be without second row Niccolò Cannone for the match in Perth. Cannone received a four-match ban following a red card during their 47-17 loss to the All Blacks last weekend. The Azzurri, who recently showed significant attacking intent in Wellington, will now need to reshuffle their pack as they prepare to face the Wallabies on Saturday.

sections.heading: Wallabies seek first win of campaign
sections.body: Australia enters the third round sitting 10th on the log with two losses from two matches. After a difficult result against France, the squad is under pressure to rectify a record that has seen them lose nine of their past 10 Tests. A victory in Perth is essential to shift momentum before the remainder of the 2026 Nations Championship schedule.

sections.heading: Selection process follows Ireland defeat
sections.body: Japan are regrouping following their 20-36 loss to Ireland last weekend. Despite the result, the team showed resilience with tries from Taira and Hayate Era, alongside consistent kicking from Matsunaga. With the squad currently sitting ninth in the Nations Championship log, coaching staff are expected to review personnel options this week to address the set-piece and defensive lapses exposed during the second round.

sections.heading: Recovery focus for Japan squad
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage following the match against Ireland. The team is currently prioritising player conditioning and recovery sessions as they transition into the training week. Staff remain focused on ensuring the squad is in peak physical condition before turning their tactical attention to the upcoming challenge against the French side.

sections.heading: France face Japan without Penaud
sections.body: France travel to face Japan following a high-scoring 42-26 victory over Australia in their recent encounter. The French side will be without winger Damian Penaud, who has been ruled out of the remaining Nations Championship matches after sustaining a calf injury during their tournament opener. France enters the fixture bolstered by a versatile attack that saw six different try-scorers touch down against the Wallabies.

sections.heading: Mid-table pressure mounts
sections.body: Japan sits ninth on the log with one win and one loss, holding a points difference of plus 1. A result against France is crucial to climbing the standings, as the race for spots in the finals weekend intensifies. With the tournament structure rewarding consistency, Japan will look to capitalise on their performance against Italy to stay competitive in the northern hemisphere table.

sections.heading: Rennie wary of Irish threat as preparations intensify
sections.body: New Zealand head coach Dave Rennie has confirmed his side remains vigilant ahead of the upcoming showdown at Eden Park. While New Zealand carries a 52-game unbeaten record at the venue, Rennie noted that his staff is "well aware of Ireland's ability" to challenge them. The squad is currently focusing on tactical adjustments to stifle the visitors’ game plan during their final training sessions.

sections.heading: Squad maintains full intensity in Auckland
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news to report from the New Zealand camp following the victory over Italy. The team continues to prioritise high-intensity training sessions at their Auckland base as they sharpen their preparations for the weekend. The squad is focused on maintaining their momentum, with coaching staff reporting a strong level of physical conditioning across the entire group heading into the final stages.

sections.heading: Ireland reports clean bill of health with key returnees
sections.body: Ireland arrives at Eden Park with a fully fit 36-man squad. Assistant coach Andrew Goodman confirmed that winger Rob Baloucoune and prop Jeremy Loughman have returned to training and are eligible for selection. Ireland enters the contest with positive momentum after securing recent wins against Australia and Japan, as they look to dismantle the long-standing unbeaten streak at New Zealand’s primary fortress.

sections.heading: Table-topping All Blacks look to consolidate position
sections.body: New Zealand sits in second place on the Nations Championship log with 81 points scored across two matches. A result this weekend is critical to maintaining their momentum at the top of the standings. With both nations unbeaten in the current competition, this fixture serves as a major test for the All Blacks’ long-standing unbeaten record at their iconic Auckland home venue.

sections.heading: Four debutants confirmed for Durban
sections.body: Rassie Erasmus has opted for a mix of established leadership and fresh talent for Saturday’s Test in Durban. Pieter-Steph du Toit will again captain the side, while uncapped fly-half Vusi Moyo is set to make his debut. The match-day squad includes a total of four debutants, reflecting the coach's intent to test depth while maintaining the standard of a perfect opening campaign.

sections.heading: Conditioning remains the focus
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage regarding the Springbok camp. The team has been focusing on conditioning and tactical preparation as they settle into their Durban base. Players continue to undergo standard recovery sessions following the physical demands of the victory over Scotland as they refine their game plan ahead of the third round of the Nations Championship.

sections.heading: Wales face significant selection hurdles
sections.body: Wales are battling mounting uncertainty as they prepare for the Durban Test. Following a reported injury to captain Dewi Lake, the squad has been forced to call up hooker Elliot Dee and forward Freddie Thomas as cover. Forwards coach Danny Wilson stated that his team is "bouncing for opportunity," though they face a formidable challenge against the current leaders of the southern hemisphere standings.

sections.heading: Maintaining momentum at the top
sections.body: South Africa currently sits at the top of the Nations Championship log with a perfect record of two wins from two matches. With a positive points difference of 38, the team is in a commanding position heading into the weekend. A victory against Wales would be vital in keeping the Springboks ahead of the pack as the tournament progresses toward the finals series in London.
